details:
Content/gameplay changes that touch behavior exercised by the native engine GameTests are very hard to
land and reconcile in this build-less controller environment, because of two compounding gaps:

1. Workers cannot run the engine-backed GameTests locally: the native `_game` pybind module is not
   built (submodules vstd / random-dungeon-generator unfetchable), so test.py GameTest cases only run
   in GitHub Actions. Workers validate with fake-game/pure-Python harnesses and pass locally, then the
   real change breaks an engine GameTest only visible in CI.
2. The CI failure is not diagnosable from the controller side: the GitHub job-log retrieval returns
   only the post-job cleanup tail (~the last ~60 lines of git-credential teardown), never the failing
   test name/assertion. The native test runner reports "[test shard 2] failed with exit code 1" but the
   specific failing GameTest and its assertion are mid-log and unreachable, so the controller cannot
   point a worker at the exact test to fix.

Combined effect: changes iterate "blind" through ~10-20 min native-CI cycles. Concrete cases this run:
- [EPIC_60][STORY_02][SUBSTORY_06] (failed-craft cost): linux failed on an engine GameTest
  (test_crafting_transactions_are_atomic RNG-failure section); only diagnosed by manually reading every
  crafting test in test.py and guessing, then reconciling. Landed after 1 extra blind cycle.
- [EPIC_60][STORY_02][SUBSTORY_07] (crafting resale economy): both economy levers (ManaPotion power
  nerf; brew_mana_potion gold raise) broke different engine crafting GameTests in shard 2 that could
  not be identified from the tail-only log after 3 cycles; the row was published BLOCKED.

Recommendation (for an optimizer with native-build access): either (a) make the native test runner emit
the failing test id + assertion into the job summary / a junit xml artifact that the controller log
tooling can read, or (b) provide a controller-runnable native-test path (prebuilt _game module or a
fetchable submodule mirror) so workers can reproduce engine GameTest failures locally before PR.

cases.ci: linux failed on test_crafting_transactions_are_atomic (engine GameTest); diagnosed manually by reading test.py; landed after extra blind cycle
cases.issue: [EPIC_60][STORY_02][SUBSTORY_06]Failed crafts consume no ingredients or gold

cases.ci: linux failed on an unidentifiable engine crafting GameTest in [test shard 2] across 3 cycles; BLOCKED

gaps: job-log API returns only cleanup tail, not the failing test/assertion

fingerprint: engine gametest failure undiagnosable build-less tail-only ci logs
impact: Content/gameplay changes iterate blind through long native-CI cycles; row e60-s02-ss07 could not be diagnosed and was BLOCKED
summary: Engine GameTest failures are undiagnosable from the controller (no local _game build + tail-only CI logs), forcing blind iterations and a BLOCKED
